headline: Hands Free, AIs Forward: NVIDIA XR AI Brings Agents to AR Glasses
article_title: Hands Free, AIs Forward: NVIDIA XR AI Brings Agents to AR Glasses

article_items.title: Hands Free, AIs Forward: NVIDIA XR AI Brings Agents to AR Glasses
article_items.description: NVIDIA XR AI is now available in public beta, giving developers a framework for building multimodal AI agents for AR glasses and XR devices.
article_items.excerpt: NVIDIA XR AI is now available in public beta, giving developers a framework for building multimodal AI agents for AR glasses and XR devices.
article_items.summary: NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit enables tool use, reasoning workflows and multi-agent coordination, while NVIDIA accelerated computing platforms - including NVIDIA DGX Spark, NVIDIA DGX Station and NVIDIA RTX PRO systems - provide the infrastructure to run inference across cloud, data center and edge environments. By connecting inputs from AR glasses and XR devices with AI models, enterprise data, tools and accelerated computing, NVIDIA XR AI enables agents that can perceive, reason and act in the flow of work. In the research lab, Rana , an AutoBio company building AI systems for scientific research, is introducing its LabOS system on NVIDIA XR AI to bring spatial intelligence directly into scientific workflows. Integrates agent orchestration and accelerated runtime services to help developers move from prototype to production. Like human workers, they need knowledge, tools and specialized skills to perceive and understand the world through video, audio and sensor data, interpret fast-changing conditions and spatial context, retrieve information from enterprise systems, reason about the next best action and use software tools to complete.

It provides a foundation for developers to build or connect skills and tools for enterprise XR applications, and simplifies the integration of multimodal perception, enterprise retrieval, reasoning models and agent.

Supports a broad ecosystem of AI models, including NVIDIA Nemotron reasoning models, NVIDIA Cosmos Reason and other compatible foundation models.
Integrates agent orchestration and accelerated runtime services to help developers move from prototype to production.

Ingests real-world signals from AR and XR devices, including video, audio, depth, pose and sensor data.

Industries Put NVIDIA XR AI to Work Across manufacturing, science, healthcare, design and immersive learning, developers and enterprises are already tapping NVIDIA XR AI - embedding AI agents where the work happens.
VITURE integrated NVIDIA XR AI into a wearable interface that gives workers a hands-free way to find the right context and guide the next step at the point of work.

article_items.title: Kraken Launches Pre-IPO Perps For OpenAI And Anthropic With Up To 5x Leverage | Bitcoinist.com
article_items.description: Kraken has launched pre-IPO perpetual futures tied to OpenAI and Anthropic, giving eligible traders leveraged exposure to private AI companies before public listings.
article_items.excerpt: Kraken has launched pre-IPO perpetual futures tied to OpenAI and Anthropic, giving eligible traders leveraged exposure to private AI companies before public listings.
article_items.summary: Kraken Launches Pre-IPO Perps For OpenAI And Anthropic With Up To 5x Leverage Kraken is pushing deeper into the overlap between crypto derivatives and private-market exposure with the launch of pre-IPO perpetual futures tied to OpenAI and Anthropic. The exchange said the new contracts allow eligible traders to take long or short positions on two of the most closely watched private artificial intelligence companies before any public listing. Why This Launch Matters The launch shows how crypto trading infrastructure is expanding beyond tokens. Kraken has launched pre-IPO perpetual futures for OpenAI and Anthropic. That could be powerful, but it also raises questions about investor protection, disclosures, pricing sources, and liquidity depth. article_items.title: AI could help win ‘race against extinction’ of vital plants, say botanists
article_items.description: Tech is helping to identify and save new specimens and could open ‘genomic goldmine’ of fungi data
article_items.excerpt: Tech is helping to identify and save new specimens and could open ‘genomic goldmine’ of fungi data
article_items.summary: OpenAI’s boss, Sam Altman, said in February: “It also takes a lot of energy to train a human.” The report, produced by 400 scientists across 40 countries, cautions that using digitisation and AI could amplify existing biases and inequalities unless the underlying data is expanded and improved. New technology is enabling scientists to track how flowering times have shifted by weeks around the world, rapidly identify new specimens and even get crucial genetic data from 180-year-old fungus specimens, potentially. key_points: OpenAI’s boss, Sam Altman, said in February: “It also takes a lot of energy to train a human.” The report, produced by 400 scientists across 40 countries, cautions that using digitisation and AI could amplify existing.

New technology is enabling scientists to track how flowering times have shifted by weeks around the world, rapidly identify new specimens and even get crucial genetic data from 180-year-old fungus specimens, potentially.
The Guardian reported in May that datacentres are now consuming 6% of electricity in the UK and US.
It called for partnerships between the technology companies and environment organisations and for governments and funders to invest in plant and fungi collections.
The situation is even more stark for fungi, with 90% of an estimated 2m species still unknown to science and less than 1% of known species assessed for extinction risk.
The researchers said the breakthrough makes historical fungarium specimens a “genomic goldmine” for new medicines and the prediction of disease outbreaks.
For example, a study using herbarium specimens showed that about 80% of the kindal trees in India’s Western Ghats , which are important for timber, used to flower at the same time.
In total, there are 145m digital specimens now online globally, but this is less than 16% of the total held in herbariums, leaving “huge blind spots in understanding”, the scientists said.
It revealed flowering has shifted by an average 2.5 days a decade over the last century due to the climate crisis.
About 2,000 new plant species are recorded each year but this “barely scratches the surface” said Prof Alexandre Antonelli, the executive director of science at RBG Kew.

Tech is helping to identify and save new specimens and could open ‘genomic goldmine’ of fungi data Support the Guardian AI could help win ‘race against extinction’ of vital plants, say botanists Tech is helping to.

Digitising images and collection data of plant and fungi specimens also speeds up international collaboration and can open up important but rarely accessed collections in biodiversity hotspots, such as Madagascar.

article_items.title: Xreal officially reveals its Android XR Aura glasses — without a pricetag - Engadget
article_items.description: The company is taking paid reservations for a launch later this year.
article_items.excerpt: The company is taking paid reservations for a launch later this year.
article_items.summary: The company is taking paid reservations for a launch later this year. Xreal officially reveals its Android XR Aura glasses - without a pricetag The company is taking paid reservations for a launch later this year. And the company has clearly invested on the content side, announcing a slew of new experiences coming to Aura. Xreal's first Android XR glasses will go on sale later this year, the company announced at the Augmented World Expo event in Long Beach, California. And I fully expect Aura to be much more expensive than any other glasses it makes given all the extra tech involved (its OnePro frames, by comparison, cost $650). Xreal says it's "targeting" the initial "wave" of products to launch in the fall in the US, UK, Japan, Canada and South Korea with European availability.

key_points: The company is taking paid reservations for a launch later this year.
Xreal officially reveals its Android XR Aura glasses - without a pricetag The company is taking paid reservations for a launch later this year.

Instead, it's opening up a two-week reservation period during which you can pay $99 to reserve a pair that will come with a $199 "launch credit." That will effectively save you $100 when it actually goes on sale.

Xreal says it's "targeting" the initial "wave" of products to launch in the fall in the US, UK, Japan, Canada and South Korea with European availability "coming soon after." Do not sell or share my personal information.
And the company has clearly invested on the content side, announcing a slew of new experiences coming to Aura.
Xreal is also offering a limited number of "founder priority pass" reservations that cost $299 in exchange for "guaranteed launch-day delivery" and special edition hardware.
Xreal's first Android XR glasses will go on sale later this year, the company announced at the Augmented World Expo event in Long Beach, California.

With a 70-degree field of view, built-in hand tracking and Qualcomm's new Snapdragon Reality Elite chip, the frames felt like a powerful middle ground between a bulky headset and a true "glasses" experience.
It's also not entirely clear when people who pay for a reservation will get one, since that "guaranteed launch day" still doesn't have an actual date.
And I fully expect Aura to be much more expensive than any other glasses it makes given all the extra tech involved (its OnePro frames, by comparison, cost $650).
Xreal might be hoping it can capitalize on the lack of other Android XR devices at the moment.
Despite AWE marking the glasses' "official" debut, the company hasn't shared a final price.
